query_used: Condition: Subacute shoulder impingement syndrome | Stage: Moderate | Symptoms: dull ache in outer part of shoulder | Onset: few days | Functional Limitations: slight difficulty performing some daily activities, such as lifting my arm above shoulder level, reaching for objects on high shelves, or carrying heavier items

final_llm_response: {"summary": "Adhesive capsulitis is a common condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, particularly at the end ranges of motion. Medical screening should be performed to rule out other causes of shoulder pain, such as rotator cuff tendinitis or glenohumeral arthritis. Differential diagnosis is important for guiding treatment decisions. Conservative management typically involves physical therapy and anti-inflammatory medication. Surgery may be considered in severe cases.", "insights": "The clinical presentation of adhesive capsulitis suggests a peripheral nerve entrapment, likely due to repetitive overhead movements or trauma. The condition can mimic rotator cuff tendinitis and glenohumeral arthritis, making differential diagnosis crucial. Conservative management typically involves physical therapy and anti-inflammatory medication. Surgery may be considered in severe cases.", "treatment_steps": ["Conservative management with physical therapy and anti-inflammatory medication", "Surgical intervention in severe cases"], "sources_used": ["kelley-et-al-2013-shoulder-pain-and-mobility-deficits-adhesive-capsulitis_chunks_1.json"], "disclaimer": "This is a computer-generated clinical insight, not a replacement for professional medical advice."}
